AI/ML.text: Concentration of Power in AI is a Risk, Not a Solution  “The answer to concentration of power in AI is not openness at all costs, but a serious research commons with the resources to compete”  > The piece warns that a "permissioned frontier" where access depends…

Concentration of Power in AI is a Risk, Not a Solution
“The answer to concentration of power in AI is not openness at all costs, but a serious research commons with the resources to compete”
> The piece warns that a "permissioned frontier" where access depends on joining closed institutions undermines innovation, competition, and legitimate governance.
> It calls for a well-funded open AI research commons with frontier-scale compute and resources, enabling universities and independent researchers to compete while keeping AI development more open, collaborative, and broadly distributed.
Source:
https://x.com/andykonwinski/status/2072830533739192560

Microsoft Introduces Frontier Company
A new operating business focused on delivering Frontier Transformation through AI for all users.
> It will provide a unique combination of skills inclusive of deep industry knowledge, change management, continuous improvement experience, and enterprise-grade AI engineering expertise.
> To help enterprise build its own AI capability, and create a frontier ecosystem where every business can turn its knowledge, workflows, and judgment into its own AI systems that continuously improve.
Source:
blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2026/07/02/microsoft-frontier-company-ai-engineering-that-amplifies-and-protects-your-intelligence/

The Case for Decentralized AI Training in Space
“If AI training is becoming constrained by Earth’s geography, then the next frontier is not just bigger terrestrial clusters. It is distributed training across a new topology: ground data centers, edge devices, satellites, orbital compute nodes, & decentralized protocols all stitched into one learning network”
> Decentralized AI training in space entails satellites and orbital compute nodes not only running inference, but participating in training, fine-tuning, post-training, and eventually larger-scale model updates across a dynamic orbital network.
Source:

The next frontier for AI training is shifting away from resource-constrained terrestrial clusters toward a decentralized, ground-orbit hybrid network where a dynamic graph of satellites and orbital compute nodes collaborate to update models in space

Meta Compute: Everyone Wants To Be A Neocloud
Meta is rapidly accelerating its datacenter and compute procurement - contracting over 5GW of capacity in the first half of 2026 alone - to create high-margin optionality across frontier AI models, expanded recommendation systems, "SpaceX-type" short-term compute rentals, and a potential "Bedrock-type" token-as-a-service partnership with Anthropic
https://newsletter.semianalysis.com/p/meta-compute-everyone-wants-to-be

Benchmarks Are the Best Business in Finance. Compute Is Next
Volatile GPU compute market is currently repeating the financial playbook of credit indices like CDX and iTraxx by establishing neutral reference benchmarks to capture high-margin, asset-light market dominance
https://0xmoonletter.substack.com/p/benchmarks-are-the-best-business

The Secret History of Polymarket – Part 1
This article explores the origins of Polymarket, suggesting it is a privatized rebranding of the Pentagon's controversial, defunct "Terrorism Futures Market" (the Policy Analysis Market)
Backed by prominent tech billionaires, platforms like Polymarket and Kalshi are expanding into mainstream finance and politics while facing growing concerns over market manipulation and insider trading
https://unlimitedhangout.com/2026/06/investigative-series/the-secret-history-of-polymarket-part-1/

MYSTERIOUS SOLANA PROJECT WORLD UNVEILED AS FULLY ONCHAIN PREDICTION MARKET

The World as Model - on sensing, representations, and the financialization of the physical world
Just as high-frequency trading firms won by building high-fidelity representations of market data, a new generation of companies is capturing physical infrastructure at its native resolution to create a digital representation layer
Mapping reality through physics-informed models instead of linguistic bottlenecks allows these platforms to accurately predict asset behavior and reshape how trillions of dollars in industrial assets are financed, insured, and traded
https://cruciblecapital.substack.com/p/the-world-as-model

> Anthropic has increased Claude API rate limits for all users, simplified tier progression by removing API spend requirements, and boosted top-tier Sonnet and Haiku limits by up to 5×.

If the Founder Cared About His Token, it’d Be 10x the Price
> Protocols with both equity and tokens often see equity prioritized over token holders, causing tokens to trade at steep discounts despite strong underlying businesses.
> Founders running dual equity-and-token structures are implicitly incentivized to prioritize equity, which is legally cleaner, easier to raise on, and better understood by investors.
> The proposed fix is for protocols to stop selling tokens to retail while reserving equity for VCs, and instead align tokenholder rights more closely with equity so the token isn't just a memecoin wearing a profitable business's logo.
Source:

CT Debates Risks and Benefits of Doxxing
For Doxxing:
> Undoxxed with no reason is seen as outdated in 2026.
> Builders, content creators, and career-focused people benefit from visibility, credibility, and networking.
> Founders hiding identity raises trust concerns; doxxing signals accountability.
> Helps with onboarding mainstream audiences through traditional platforms like YouTube/Instagram.
Against Doxxing:
> Increases the risk of targeted theft, extortion, kidnapping
> Being your own bank is only a real advantage if you stay anonymous; a known face makes you a target
> Database leaks are growing and AI tools make identity exposure increasingly dangerous over time
> Even being publicly known in any capacity makes you a physical target, risk no longer worth reward.

Take RWA Tokenization Offshore First
> Financial institutions should pursue offshore RWA tokenization rather than wait for local regulation, using established jurisdictions or onchain-native platforms to gain experience and advantages.
> Success hangs on six areas: jurisdiction, licensing, asset type, investor scope, settlement currency, and operations, with legal review being the single biggest bottleneck in any route taken.
Source:
https://x.com/tiger_research_/status/2072868366315069525

On bear:

The Secret History of Polymarket – Part 1
This article explores the origins of Polymarket, suggesting it is a privatized rebranding of the Pentagon's controversial, defunct "Terrorism Futures Market" (the Policy Analysis Market)
Backed by prominent tech billionaires, platforms like Polymarket and Kalshi are expanding into mainstream finance and politics while facing growing concerns over market manipulation and insider trading
https://unlimitedhangout.com/2026/06/investigative-series/the-secret-history-of-polymarket-part-1/

Venice Raises a $65M Series A at a $1B Valuation
> Venice has raised its first-round of outside capital: $65m at a valuation of $1 billion.
> The round was led by Dragonfly, alongside North Island Ventures, Coinbase Ventures, Archetype, Liquid2 Ventures, Morgan Creek, and others.
> Venice aims to construct the platform dedicated to private and unrestricted machine intelligence; an open, permissive port city that respects the sovereignty of its inhabitants, human & agentic.
Source:
https://x.com/ErikVoorhees/status/2072326370206581037

Crypto losing mindshare/trust among the mainstream audiences

Collector Crypt's $62M revenue is the same money, going in circles
Collector Crypt’s reported $62M revenue was almost entirely ($58M) recycled external deposits looped through automated buyback bots, not organic demand - evidenced by 93% volume from top wallets and metronome-like bots making identical $250 buys 20 hours/day
The vault holds real graded cards worth $18.6M and recycling was disclosed, but revenue equals the float with near-zero net value creation
https://x.com/johna2an/status/2071106458695901425?s=20

Modern tech substitutes like streaming and AI chatbots have created a synthetic form of connection, accelerating a widespread public health crisis of loneliness
Overcoming this isolation requires people to embrace the vulnerability of real-world interactions and actively rebuild genuine communities through scheduled, in-person social groups
https://jords.life/p/counterfeit-congregation

Concentration of Power in AI is a Risk, Not a Solution
“The answer to concentration of power in AI is not openness at all costs, but a serious research commons with the resources to compete”
> The piece warns that a "permissioned frontier" where access depends on joining closed institutions undermines innovation, competition, and legitimate governance.
> It calls for a well-funded open AI research commons with frontier-scale compute and resources, enabling universities and independent researchers to compete while keeping AI development more open, collaborative, and broadly distributed.
Source:
https://x.com/andykonwinski/status/2072830533739192560

The Case for Decentralized AI Training in Space
“If AI training is becoming constrained by Earth’s geography, then the next frontier is not just bigger terrestrial clusters. It is distributed training across a new topology: ground data centers, edge devices, satellites, orbital compute nodes, & decentralized protocols all stitched into one learning network”
> Decentralized AI training in space entails satellites and orbital compute nodes not only running inference, but participating in training, fine-tuning, post-training, and eventually larger-scale model updates across a dynamic orbital network.
Source:
https://jrodthoughts.medium.com/the-case-for-decentralized-ai-in-space-9141b3ad3096

The Case for Decentralized AI Training in Space
The next frontier for AI training is shifting away from resource-constrained terrestrial clusters toward a decentralized, ground-orbit hybrid network where a dynamic graph of satellites and orbital compute nodes collaborate to update models in space
https://jrodthoughts.medium.com/the-case-for-decentralized-ai-in-space-9141b3ad3096

Meta Compute: Everyone Wants To Be A Neocloud
Meta is rapidly accelerating its datacenter and compute procurement - contracting over 5GW of capacity in the first half of 2026 alone - to create high-margin optionality across frontier AI models, expanded recommendation systems, "SpaceX-type" short-term compute rentals, and a potential "Bedrock-type" token-as-a-service partnership with Anthropic
https://newsletter.semianalysis.com/p/meta-compute-everyone-wants-to-be

Benchmarks Are the Best Business in Finance. Compute Is Next
Volatile GPU compute market is currently repeating the financial playbook of credit indices like CDX and iTraxx by establishing neutral reference benchmarks to capture high-margin, asset-light market dominance
https://0xmoonletter.substack.com/p/benchmarks-are-the-best-business
